overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong and highly targeted academic presence across LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon, reaching a combined audience of 3,580 followers. His content is deeply specialized around computational social science, disinformation studies, and academic opportunities. Across all platforms, he achieves remarkably high engagement, particularly when announcing research publications, software tool updates, and recruitment opportunities, showing a highly active and professional community.

contentThemes.description: Research updates, paper publications, and methodology discussions related to online information operations.
Recruitment drives for postdoctoral researchers and research opportunities for PhD students.
Updates on software releases and technical improvements to data detection tools like CooRnet.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's exceptionally high relative engagement to drive applications for PhD opportunities and paper reads.
Add visual elements, such as diagrams, screenshots of CooRnet v2.0 in action, or graphical abstracts of papers to boost engagement further, especially on LinkedIn.
Encourage deeper interaction on Mastodon by asking open-ended methodological questions to stimulate discussion in academic circles.
Use LinkedIn specifically for high-level professional milestones and hiring, while reserving Bluesky and Mastodon for real-time academic community discussions and tool usage tips.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky yields the highest relative engagement rate (6.14% average), showing an incredibly responsive niche audience for both publication announcements and student outreach.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides steady, focused engagement (4.55% average) centered on specialized research tools and methodology, making it ideal for technical feedback and academic networking.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn acts as the primary professional driver with the largest absolute audience (1,850 followers), achieving high absolute volumes of interactions for organizational milestones, grant acquisitions, and hiring announcements.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
